findings.description: The NC Energy, Mineral & Land Resources logo/header graphic is present in both versions but the HTML version appears slightly different in sizing/positioning.
findings.evidence: PDF shows the NC logo with 'Energy, Mineral & Land Resources' and 'ENVIRONMENTAL QUALITY' text beneath it on the left, with officials' names on the right. HTML preserves the same layout but with minor sizing differences.

findings.description: The memo header fields (To, From, Subject) have slightly different formatting in HTML versus PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF uses a more structured memo format with clear field labels and indented values. HTML renders them as separate lines without the same indentation alignment, though content is preserved.

findings.description: The outline list (I through IV) has slightly different indentation and formatting in the HTML version.
findings.evidence: PDF shows Roman numerals with tab-aligned text in a structured list format. HTML shows them with bullet-style indentation that is slightly different but still readable.

findings.description: Section heading 'I. History/Background' uses slightly different styling between PDF and HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF uses bold Roman numeral with tab-separated bold heading. HTML uses a larger heading style for the same content.

findings.description: Section heading 'II. March 14, 2016 Public Meeting and Oral Comments Summary' uses a larger heading style in HTML compared to PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF has a bold inline heading. HTML renders it as a larger, more prominent heading element.

findings.description: Bold topic labels within bullet points are preserved but the colon usage and formatting differ slightly.
findings.evidence: PDF shows 'Environmental:' with bold and colon formatting. HTML preserves the bold but styling is slightly different.

findings.description: Section III heading 'Written Public Comment Summary' uses a larger heading style in HTML.
findings.evidence: PDF uses bold inline heading with Roman numeral. HTML renders as a larger standalone heading element.

findings.description: Section IV 'Attachments' heading and the attachment list have slightly different formatting.
findings.evidence: PDF uses Roman numerals (I through VI) with tab-aligned descriptions. HTML uses a similar numbered list but with slightly different indentation style.

findings.description: The document title 'MEMORANDUM' is rendered as h1, which is acceptable. However, the main section headings (I, II, III, IV) are rendered as h2, which is appropriate. No hierarchy skipping issues found. Minor note: the h1 has underline styling via <u> tag matching the PDF's underlined title.
findings.evidence: h1: MEMORANDUM, h2: I. History/Background, h2: II. March 14, 2016..., h2: III. Written Public Comment Summary, h2: IV. Attachments

findings.description: Alt text on images is generic or vague rather than accurately descriptive. The first image alt text mentions 'document background' which is somewhat misleading - it appears to be the NC state logo/letterhead. Subsequent images have empty alt text.
findings.evidence: First img: alt='North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality document background'; remaining images: alt=''

findings.description: There are nested <main> tags in the HTML (a <main> inside a <main>), which is a structural issue but does not cause content duplication. No actual content is duplicated.
